IQ Method3 Bench C 1.0.0
Documentacion tecnica profesional del pipeline IQ en C
Cargando...
Buscando...
Nada coincide
main.c
Ir a la documentación de este archivo.
1#include "iq_bench.h"
2
7
8#include <math.h>
9#include <stdio.h>
10#include <stdlib.h>
11#include <string.h>
12#include <time.h>
13
18static void print_usage(const char *exe)
19{
20 /* Keep options minimal and reproducible for benchmark comparisons. */
21 printf("Usage:\n");
22 printf(" %s [--db <path>] [--files <N>] [--max-complex <N>] [--nperseg <N>] [--overlap <0..0.9>] [--chunk-bytes <N>] [--json-out <file>] [--csv-out <file>]\n", exe);
23 printf("\nDefaults:\n");
24 printf(" --db D:/wnOs/wsp/CODE/work/PersonalStuff/GCPDS/ANE2/maintenance/qualityAssurance/modules/db/DataBase-IQ-FM-88MHz-108MHz\n");
25 printf(" --files 6\n");
26 printf(" --max-complex 262144\n");
27 printf(" --nperseg 512\n");
28 printf(" --overlap 0.5\n");
29 printf(" --chunk-bytes 65536\n");
30}
31
59static int write_json_summary(const char *path,
60 const perf_timing_t *t,
61 double total_samples,
62 double expected_samples,
63 double throughput,
64 double throughput_eff,
65 double drop_rate_pct,
66 double cpu_ms,
67 double cpu_percent,
68 double ram_percent,
69 double rss_peak,
70 double avg_file_ms,
71 double file_jitter_ms,
72 double latency_ratio,
73 double mean_snr,
74 double std_snr,
75 double mean_noise,
76 double mean_center,
77 double score,
78 double rss_start,
79 double rss_end,
80 int files_ok,
81 int files_req)
82{
83 FILE *f = fopen(path, "w");
84 if (!f)
85 {
86 return 0;
87 }
88
89 fprintf(f, "{\n");
90 fprintf(f, " \"files_requested\": %d,\n", files_req);
91 fprintf(f, " \"files_processed\": %d,\n", files_ok);
92 fprintf(f, " \"samples_total\": %.0f,\n", total_samples);
93 fprintf(f, " \"samples_expected\": %.0f,\n", expected_samples);
94 fprintf(f, " \"throughput_sps\": %.6f,\n", throughput);
95 fprintf(f, " \"throughput_efficiency\": %.6f,\n", throughput_eff);
96 fprintf(f, " \"drop_rate_pct\": %.6f,\n", drop_rate_pct);
97 fprintf(f, " \"cpu_ms\": %.6f,\n", cpu_ms);
98 fprintf(f, " \"cpu_percent\": %.6f,\n", cpu_percent);
99 fprintf(f, " \"ram_percent\": %.6f,\n", ram_percent);
100 fprintf(f, " \"rss_peak_mb\": %.6f,\n", rss_peak);
101 fprintf(f, " \"avg_file_ms\": %.6f,\n", avg_file_ms);
102 fprintf(f, " \"file_jitter_ms\": %.6f,\n", file_jitter_ms);
103 fprintf(f, " \"latency_ratio\": %.6f,\n", latency_ratio);
104 fprintf(f, " \"total_ms\": %.6f,\n", t->total_ms);
105 fprintf(f, " \"load_ms\": %.6f,\n", t->load_ms);
106 fprintf(f, " \"convert_ms\": %.6f,\n", t->convert_ms);
107 fprintf(f, " \"preprocess_ms\": %.6f,\n", t->preprocess_ms);
108 fprintf(f, " \"welch_ms\": %.6f,\n", t->welch_ms);
109 fprintf(f, " \"metric_ms\": %.6f,\n", t->metric_ms);
110 fprintf(f, " \"snr_mean_db\": %.6f,\n", mean_snr);
111 fprintf(f, " \"snr_std_db\": %.6f,\n", std_snr);
112 fprintf(f, " \"noise_mean_dbm\": %.6f,\n", mean_noise);
113 fprintf(f, " \"center_mean_dbm\": %.6f,\n", mean_center);
114 fprintf(f, " \"score\": %.6f,\n", score);
115 fprintf(f, " \"rss_start_mb\": %.6f,\n", rss_start);
116 fprintf(f, " \"rss_end_mb\": %.6f\n", rss_end);
117 fprintf(f, "}\n");
118
119 fclose(f);
120 return 1;
121}
122
150static int write_csv_summary(const char *path,
151 const perf_timing_t *t,
152 double total_samples,
153 double expected_samples,
154 double throughput,
155 double throughput_eff,
156 double drop_rate_pct,
157 double cpu_ms,
158 double cpu_percent,
159 double ram_percent,
160 double rss_peak,
161 double avg_file_ms,
162 double file_jitter_ms,
163 double latency_ratio,
164 double mean_snr,
165 double std_snr,
166 double mean_noise,
167 double mean_center,
168 double score,
169 double rss_start,
170 double rss_end,
171 int files_ok,
172 int files_req)
173{
174 FILE *f = fopen(path, "w");
175 if (!f)
176 {
177 return 0;
178 }
179
180 fprintf(f, "files_requested,files_processed,samples_total,samples_expected,throughput_sps,throughput_efficiency,drop_rate_pct,cpu_ms,cpu_percent,ram_percent,rss_peak_mb,avg_file_ms,file_jitter_ms,latency_ratio,total_ms,load_ms,convert_ms,preprocess_ms,welch_ms,metric_ms,snr_mean_db,snr_std_db,noise_mean_dbm,center_mean_dbm,score,rss_start_mb,rss_end_mb\n");
181 fprintf(f, "%d,%d,%.0f,%.0f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f\n",
182 files_req, files_ok, total_samples, expected_samples, throughput, throughput_eff, drop_rate_pct,
183 cpu_ms, cpu_percent, ram_percent, rss_peak, avg_file_ms, file_jitter_ms, latency_ratio,
184 t->total_ms, t->load_ms, t->convert_ms, t->preprocess_ms, t->welch_ms, t->metric_ms,
185 mean_snr, std_snr, mean_noise, mean_center, score, rss_start, rss_end);
186
187 fclose(f);
188 return 1;
189}
190
197int main(int argc, char **argv)
198{
199 /* Defaults tuned for quick local runs; override via CLI. */
200 const char *db_dir = "D:/wnOs/wsp/CODE/work/PersonalStuff/GCPDS/ANE2/maintenance/qualityAssurance/modules/db/DataBase-IQ-FM-88MHz-108MHz";
201 int files_to_use = 6;
202 size_t max_complex = 262144;
203 int nperseg = 512;
204 float overlap = 0.5f;
205 size_t chunk_bytes = 65536;
206 const char *json_out = NULL;
207 const char *csv_out = NULL;
208
209 for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++)
210 {
211 /* Simple argument parser, fail-fast on unknown options. */
212 if (strcmp(argv[i], "--db") == 0 && i + 1 < argc)
213 {
214 db_dir = argv[++i];
215 }
216 else if (strcmp(argv[i], "--files") == 0 && i + 1 < argc)
217 {
218 files_to_use = atoi(argv[++i]);
219 }
220 else if (strcmp(argv[i], "--max-complex") == 0 && i + 1 < argc)
221 {
222 max_complex = (size_t)strtoull(argv[++i], NULL, 10);
223 }
224 else if (strcmp(argv[i], "--nperseg") == 0 && i + 1 < argc)
225 {
226 nperseg = atoi(argv[++i]);
227 }
228 else if (strcmp(argv[i], "--overlap") == 0 && i + 1 < argc)
229 {
230 overlap = (float)atof(argv[++i]);
231 }
232 else if (strcmp(argv[i], "--chunk-bytes") == 0 && i + 1 < argc)
233 {
234 chunk_bytes = (size_t)strtoull(argv[++i], NULL, 10);
235 }
236 else if (strcmp(argv[i], "--json-out") == 0 && i + 1 < argc)
237 {
238 json_out = argv[++i];
239 }
240 else if (strcmp(argv[i], "--csv-out") == 0 && i + 1 < argc)
241 {
242 csv_out = argv[++i];
243 }
244 else if (strcmp(argv[i], "--help") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "-h") == 0)
245 {
246 print_usage(argv[0]);
247 return 0;
248 }
249 else
250 {
251 printf("Unknown option: %s\n", argv[i]);
252 print_usage(argv[0]);
253 return 1;
254 }
255 }
256
257 sigmf_pair_t pairs[MAX_PAIRS];
258 int total_pairs = discover_sigmf_pairs(db_dir, pairs, MAX_PAIRS);
259
260 if (total_pairs <= 0)
261 {
262 printf("No SigMF pairs found in: %s\n", db_dir);
263 return 1;
264 }
265
266 if (files_to_use > total_pairs)
267 {
268 files_to_use = total_pairs;
269 }
270
271 mem_tracker_t mt;
272 mem_tracker_init(&mt);
273
274 int exit_code = 0;
275 int8_t *raw_buf = NULL;
276 complexf_t *iq_buf = NULL;
277 welch_workspace_t ws;
278 memset(&ws, 0, sizeof(ws));
279
280 size_t raw_capacity = max_complex * 2U;
281 raw_buf = (int8_t *)mt_alloc(&mt, raw_capacity);
282 iq_buf = (complexf_t *)mt_alloc(&mt, max_complex * sizeof(complexf_t));
283 if (!raw_buf || !iq_buf)
284 {
285 printf("[ERR] Failed to allocate reusable raw/IQ buffers\n");
286 exit_code = 2;
287 goto cleanup_all;
288 }
289
290 if (!welch_workspace_init(&ws, nperseg, overlap, &mt))
291 {
292 printf("[ERR] Welch workspace init failed (nperseg must be power-of-two and allocations must succeed)\n");
293 exit_code = 2;
294 goto cleanup_all;
295 }
296
297 perf_timing_t t = {0};
298 double total_start = now_ms();
299 double rss_start = process_rss_mb();
300 clock_t cpu_start = clock();
301
302 double sum_noise = 0.0;
303 double sum_center = 0.0;
304 double sum_snr = 0.0;
305 double sum_snr2 = 0.0;
306 double total_samples = 0.0;
307
308 printf("================================================================================\n");
309 printf("METHOD 3 C BENCHMARK (DC + RMS NORM)\n");
310 printf("================================================================================\n");
311 printf("DB: %s\n", db_dir);
312 printf("Files: %d / %d\n", files_to_use, total_pairs);
313 printf("max_complex=%llu nperseg=%d overlap=%.2f chunk_bytes=%llu\n\n", (unsigned long long)max_complex, nperseg, overlap, (unsigned long long)chunk_bytes);
314
315 int files_processed = 0;
316 double sum_file_ms = 0.0;
317 double sum_file_ms2 = 0.0;
318 double first_sample_rate = 0.0;
319
320 for (int i = 0; i < files_to_use; i++)
321 {
322 /* Single cleanup path per file stage, even with reusable buffers. */
323 sigmf_meta_t meta = {0};
324 size_t raw_bytes = 0;
325 const float *freq = NULL;
326 const float *psd = NULL;
327 size_t bins = 0;
328 iq_metrics_t m = {0};
329 int file_ok = 1;
330
331 double file_start = now_ms();
332
333 if (!parse_sigmf_meta(pairs[i].meta_path, &meta))
334 {
335 printf("[WARN] Meta parse failed: %s\n", pairs[i].meta_path);
336 file_ok = 0;
337 goto file_cleanup;
338 }
339
340 if (first_sample_rate <= 0.0)
341 {
342 first_sample_rate = (double)meta.sample_rate;
343 }
344
345 double s = now_ms();
346 if (!load_iq_int8_limited_chunked_into(pairs[i].data_path, raw_buf, raw_capacity, &raw_bytes, chunk_bytes))
347 {
348 printf("[WARN] Data load failed: %s\n", pairs[i].data_path);
349 file_ok = 0;
350 goto file_cleanup;
351 }
352 t.load_ms += now_ms() - s;
353
354 size_t n_complex = raw_bytes / 2U;
355 if (n_complex == 0 || n_complex > max_complex)
356 {
357 printf("[WARN] Invalid sample count for: %s\n", pairs[i].data_path);
358 file_ok = 0;
359 goto file_cleanup;
360 }
361 total_samples += (double)n_complex;
362
363 s = now_ms();
364 int8_to_complexf(raw_buf, raw_bytes, iq_buf, n_complex);
365 t.convert_ms += now_ms() - s;
366
367 s = now_ms();
368 method3_dc_rms_norm(iq_buf, n_complex);
369 t.preprocess_ms += now_ms() - s;
370
371 s = now_ms();
372 if (!welch_fft_shifted(iq_buf, n_complex, meta.sample_rate, &ws, &freq, &psd, &bins))
373 {
374 printf("[WARN] Welch failed for: %s\n", pairs[i].data_path);
375 file_ok = 0;
376 goto file_cleanup;
377 }
378 t.welch_ms += now_ms() - s;
379
380 s = now_ms();
381 compute_metrics_from_psd(freq, psd, bins, &m);
382 t.metric_ms += now_ms() - s;
383
384 sum_noise += m.noise_floor_dbm;
385 sum_center += m.center_power_dbm;
386 sum_snr += m.snr_center_db;
387 sum_snr2 += m.snr_center_db * m.snr_center_db;
388 files_processed++;
389 double file_ms = now_ms() - file_start;
390 sum_file_ms += file_ms;
391 sum_file_ms2 += file_ms * file_ms;
392
393 printf("%02d) SNR=%7.3f dB | NF=%8.3f dBm | CP=%8.3f dBm | file_ms=%8.2f\n",
394 i + 1, m.snr_center_db, m.noise_floor_dbm, m.center_power_dbm, file_ms);
395
396 file_cleanup:
397 if (!file_ok)
398 {
399 printf("[INFO] File skipped: %s\n", pairs[i].data_path);
400 }
401 }
402
403 t.total_ms = now_ms() - total_start;
404 double rss_end = process_rss_mb();
405 clock_t cpu_end = clock();
406 double cpu_ms = ((double)(cpu_end - cpu_start) * 1000.0) / (double)CLOCKS_PER_SEC;
407
408 int n = files_processed;
409 double mean_noise = (n > 0) ? (sum_noise / n) : 0.0;
410 double mean_center = (n > 0) ? (sum_center / n) : 0.0;
411 double mean_snr = (n > 0) ? (sum_snr / n) : 0.0;
412 double var_snr = (n > 1) ? ((sum_snr2 - (sum_snr * sum_snr) / n) / (n - 1)) : 0.0;
413 if (var_snr < 0.0)
414 {
415 var_snr = 0.0;
416 }
417
418 double score = mean_snr - 2.0 * sqrt(var_snr) - (mean_noise + 90.0);
419 double throughput = (t.total_ms > 0.0) ? (total_samples / (t.total_ms / 1000.0)) : 0.0;
420 double cpu_percent = (t.total_ms > 0.0) ? (100.0 * cpu_ms / t.total_ms) : 0.0;
421 double expected_samples = (double)files_to_use * (double)max_complex;
422 double throughput_eff = (expected_samples > 0.0) ? (total_samples / expected_samples) : 0.0;
423 double drop_rate_pct = (expected_samples > 0.0) ? (100.0 * (expected_samples - total_samples) / expected_samples) : 0.0;
424 if (drop_rate_pct < 0.0)
425 {
426 drop_rate_pct = 0.0;
427 }
428 double avg_file_ms = (files_processed > 0) ? (sum_file_ms / (double)files_processed) : 0.0;
429 double var_file_ms = (files_processed > 1) ? ((sum_file_ms2 - (sum_file_ms * sum_file_ms) / (double)files_processed) / (double)(files_processed - 1)) : 0.0;
430 if (var_file_ms < 0.0)
431 {
432 var_file_ms = 0.0;
433 }
434 double file_jitter_ms = sqrt(var_file_ms);
435 double chunk_duration_ms = (first_sample_rate > 0.0) ? ((1000.0 * (double)max_complex) / first_sample_rate) : 0.0;
436 double latency_ratio = (chunk_duration_ms > 0.0) ? (avg_file_ms / chunk_duration_ms) : 0.0;
437 double ram_percent = system_ram_percent();
438 double rss_peak = process_peak_rss_mb();
439
440 printf("\n================================================================================\n");
441 printf("SUMMARY\n");
442 printf("================================================================================\n");
443 printf("total_ms : %.3f\n", t.total_ms);
444 printf("load_ms : %.3f\n", t.load_ms);
445 printf("convert_ms : %.3f\n", t.convert_ms);
446 printf("preprocess_ms : %.3f\n", t.preprocess_ms);
447 printf("welch_ms : %.3f\n", t.welch_ms);
448 printf("metric_ms : %.3f\n", t.metric_ms);
449 printf("files_processed : %d\n", files_processed);
450 printf("samples_total : %.0f\n", total_samples);
451 printf("throughput_sps : %.2f\n", throughput);
452 printf("throughput_eff : %.2f %%\n", throughput_eff * 100.0);
453 printf("drop_rate_pct : %.4f %%\n", drop_rate_pct);
454 printf("cpu_ms : %.3f\n", cpu_ms);
455 printf("cpu_percent : %.2f\n", cpu_percent);
456 printf("ram_percent : %.2f %%\n", ram_percent);
457 printf("rss_peak_mb : %.3f\n", rss_peak);
458 printf("avg_file_ms : %.3f\n", avg_file_ms);
459 printf("file_jitter_ms : %.3f\n", file_jitter_ms);
460 printf("latency_ratio : %.3f x_chunk\n", latency_ratio);
461 printf("snr_mean_db : %.4f\n", mean_snr);
462 printf("snr_std_db : %.4f\n", sqrt(var_snr));
463 printf("noise_mean_dbm : %.4f\n", mean_noise);
464 printf("center_mean_dbm : %.4f\n", mean_center);
465 printf("score : %.4f\n", score);
466 printf("rss_start_mb : %.3f\n", rss_start);
467 printf("rss_end_mb : %.3f\n", rss_end);
468 printf("rss_delta_mb : %.3f\n", rss_end - rss_start);
469
470 /* Exact key block for terminal copy/paste and downstream QA parsers. */
471 printf("\nCOMPUTATIONAL_METRICS\n");
472 printf("total_ms: %.4f\n", t.total_ms);
473 printf("throughput_sps: %.5f\n", throughput);
474 printf("cpu_percent: %.6f\n", cpu_percent);
475 printf("ram_percent: %.1f\n", ram_percent);
476 printf("rss_peak_mb: %.6f\n", rss_peak);
477 printf("throughput_efficiency: %.6f\n", throughput_eff);
478 printf("drop_rate_pct: %.6f\n", drop_rate_pct);
479 printf("avg_file_ms: %.5f\n", avg_file_ms);
480 printf("file_jitter_ms: %.5f\n", file_jitter_ms);
481 printf("latency_ratio: %.5f\n", latency_ratio);
482 printf("snr_mean_db: %.6f\n", mean_snr);
483
484 printf("\nComponent share:\n");
485 printf("- Load %8.2f %%\n", (t.total_ms > 0.0) ? (100.0 * t.load_ms / t.total_ms) : 0.0);
486 printf("- Convert %8.2f %%\n", (t.total_ms > 0.0) ? (100.0 * t.convert_ms / t.total_ms) : 0.0);
487 printf("- Preproc %8.2f %%\n", (t.total_ms > 0.0) ? (100.0 * t.preprocess_ms / t.total_ms) : 0.0);
488 printf("- Welch %8.2f %%\n", (t.total_ms > 0.0) ? (100.0 * t.welch_ms / t.total_ms) : 0.0);
489 printf("- Metric %8.2f %%\n", (t.total_ms > 0.0) ? (100.0 * t.metric_ms / t.total_ms) : 0.0);
490
491 if (json_out)
492 {
493 if (!write_json_summary(json_out, &t, total_samples, expected_samples, throughput, throughput_eff, drop_rate_pct, cpu_ms, cpu_percent, ram_percent, rss_peak, avg_file_ms, file_jitter_ms, latency_ratio, mean_snr, sqrt(var_snr), mean_noise, mean_center, score, rss_start, rss_end, files_processed, files_to_use))
494 {
495 printf("[WARN] Failed to write JSON summary: %s\n", json_out);
496 }
497 }
498
499 if (csv_out)
500 {
501 if (!write_csv_summary(csv_out, &t, total_samples, expected_samples, throughput, throughput_eff, drop_rate_pct, cpu_ms, cpu_percent, ram_percent, rss_peak, avg_file_ms, file_jitter_ms, latency_ratio, mean_snr, sqrt(var_snr), mean_noise, mean_center, score, rss_start, rss_end, files_processed, files_to_use))
502 {
503 printf("[WARN] Failed to write CSV summary: %s\n", csv_out);
504 }
505 }
506
507cleanup_all:
508 welch_workspace_free(&ws, &mt);
509 if (iq_buf)
510 {
511 mt_free(&mt, iq_buf, max_complex * sizeof(complexf_t));
512 }
513 if (raw_buf)
514 {
515 mt_free(&mt, raw_buf, raw_capacity);
516 }
517
518 printf("\nMemory tracker:\n");
519 printf("- alloc_count : %llu\n", (unsigned long long)mt.alloc_count);
520 printf("- free_count : %llu\n", (unsigned long long)mt.free_count);
521 printf("- bytes_peak : %llu\n", (unsigned long long)mt.bytes_peak);
522 printf("- bytes_current : %llu\n", (unsigned long long)mt.bytes_current);
523
524 if (mt.bytes_current != 0 || mt.alloc_count != mt.free_count)
525 {
526 printf("[LEAK BLOCKER] Allocation tracker mismatch detected.\n");
527 if (exit_code == 0)
528 {
529 exit_code = 3;
530 }
531 }
532 else
533 {
534 printf("[OK] Internal allocation tracker reports no leaks.\n");
535 }
536
537 return exit_code;
538}
iq_bench.h
Interfaz publica del benchmark C para procesamiento IQ Metodo 3.
compute_metrics_from_psd
void compute_metrics_from_psd(const float *freq_hz, const float *psd_linear, size_t bins, iq_metrics_t *out_metrics)
Calcula metricas de senal desde una PSD lineal.
Definition welch.c:282
welch_workspace_free
void welch_workspace_free(welch_workspace_t *ws, mem_tracker_t *mt)
Libera recursos asociados a un workspace Welch.
Definition welch.c:156
now_ms
double now_ms(void)
Retorna tiempo monotono en milisegundos.
Definition profiler.c:19
welch_fft_shifted
int welch_fft_shifted(const complexf_t *x, size_t n, uint32_t fs, welch_workspace_t *ws, const float **out_freq_hz, const float **out_psd_linear, size_t *out_bins)
Calcula Welch PSD y devuelve espectro/frecuencias desplazados.
Definition welch.c:202
method3_dc_rms_norm
void method3_dc_rms_norm(complexf_t *x, size_t n)
Aplica Metodo 3 (remocion DC + normalizacion RMS) en sitio.
Definition method3.c:22
int8_to_complexf
void int8_to_complexf(const int8_t *raw, size_t raw_bytes, complexf_t *out_complex, size_t out_n)
Convierte IQ int8 intercalado a complejos float.
Definition sigmf_io.c:274
load_iq_int8_limited_chunked_into
int load_iq_int8_limited_chunked_into(const char *data_path, int8_t *raw_buf, size_t raw_capacity, size_t *out_bytes, size_t chunk_bytes)
Carga IQ int8 por chunks dentro de un buffer preasignado.
Definition sigmf_io.c:202
MAX_PAIRS
#define MAX_PAIRS
Definition iq_bench.h:16
discover_sigmf_pairs
int discover_sigmf_pairs(const char *db_dir, sigmf_pair_t *out_pairs, int max_pairs)
Descubre pares SigMF data/meta dentro de un directorio.
Definition fs_utils.c:55
mt_alloc
void * mt_alloc(mem_tracker_t *mt, size_t bytes)
Reserva memoria y actualiza contadores del tracker.
Definition profiler.c:105
welch_workspace_init
int welch_workspace_init(welch_workspace_t *ws, int nperseg, float overlap, mem_tracker_t *mt)
Inicializa workspace Welch y sus buffers reutilizables.
Definition welch.c:86
process_rss_mb
double process_rss_mb(void)
Retorna RSS actual del proceso en MB.
Definition profiler.c:40
system_ram_percent
double system_ram_percent(void)
Retorna porcentaje de carga de RAM del sistema.
Definition profiler.c:73
mem_tracker_init
void mem_tracker_init(mem_tracker_t *mt)
Inicializa el tracker de memoria interno.
Definition profiler.c:90
parse_sigmf_meta
int parse_sigmf_meta(const char *meta_path, sigmf_meta_t *out_meta)
Parsea metadata SigMF y extrae frecuencia y sample rate.
Definition sigmf_io.c:63

write_csv_summary
